title: Ceviche de Camaron Shrimp Lime

seo.title: Easy Shrimp Ceviche Recipe - Authentic Mexican Style
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best authentic Mexican Shrimp Ceviche at home with fresh ingredients. This easy recipe is bursting with citrus flavor!
h2Headings: Ingredients for Authentic Ceviche de Camaron
How to Make Mexican Shrimp Ceviche
Tips for the Best Shrimp Ceviche
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of authentic Mexican shrimp ceviche with avocado, cilantro, and lime wedges.
seo.imageCaption: Bright, zesty, and refreshing: Our authentic Mexican Shrimp Ceviche is perfect for a light meal or appetizer.

description: Indulge in the vibrant, zesty flavors of an authentic Mexican Shrimp Ceviche, a refreshing seafood delight. This easy recipe features succulent shrimp 'cooked' in fresh lime juice, perfectly balanced with crisp vegetables like tomato, onion, and cilantro, creating a beloved dish known for its bright, tangy, and slightly spicy kick.

ingredients.item: Fresh raw shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredients.notes: medium to large size, tails off
finely minced, seeds removed for less heat (optional)

instructions.title: Prepare the Shrimp
instructions.text: Start by thoroughly cleaning your shrimp. Peel and devein all the shrimp, then rinse them under cold water. Pat them completely dry with paper towels to ensure the lime juice can penetrate effectively. Cut the shrimp into 1/2-inch pieces; this allows for quicker 'cooking' and easier eating.

instructions.title: Marinate the Shrimp in Lime Juice
instructions.text: Place the diced shrimp in a non-reactive bowl (glass or ceramic). Pour the fresh lime juice over the shrimp, ensuring they are fully submerged. Stir gently to coat all pieces. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30-45 minutes, or until the shrimp turn opaque and pink, indicating they are 'cooked' by the acidity of the lime. Stir occasionally to ensure even marination.

instructions.title: Prepare the Vegetables
instructions.text: While the shrimp marinates, prepare your fresh vegetables. Finely dice the red onion, Roma tomatoes (after seeding them to prevent excess moisture), and cucumber (peeled and seeded). Finely chop the fresh cilantro and mince the serrano pepper. Remember to adjust the amount of serrano to your preferred spice level.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Once the shrimp is fully 'cooked' and opaque, drain about half of the excess lime juice from the bowl, leaving enough to keep the mixture moist and flavorful. Add the diced red onion, tomatoes, cucumber, cilantro, and minced serrano pepper to the shrimp. Gently stir all the ingredients together until well combined.

instructions.title: Season and Chill
instructions.text: Season the ceviche generously with salt and black pepper to taste. Mix well, then taste and adjust seasonings if necessary. For best results, cover the bowl again and refrigerate the Shrimp Ceviche for another 15-20 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld beautifully and ensures a perfectly chilled serving temperature.

instructions.title: Serve Fresh
instructions.text: Just before serving, dice the fresh avocado and gently fold it into the ceviche, or serve it as a garnish on top. Serve your authentic Mexican Shrimp Ceviche immediately with crispy tostadas or tortilla chips. Garnish with extra cilantro sprigs or lime wedges if desired for an inviting presentation.

tips: Always use the freshest possible shrimp and limes. The quality of these two ingredients is paramount to the authentic flavor of Mexican ceviche.

To ensure even 'cooking' and prevent mushy shrimp, cut all shrimp pieces to a uniform size. For a milder onion flavor, rinse diced red onion under cold water for a minute, then drain thoroughly before adding to the ceviche.

Ceviche is best enjoyed fresh. While it can be made a few hours ahead, avoid making it the day before as the shrimp texture can become rubbery and the vegetables may get too soft. Add avocado just before serving to prevent browning.
article:
## Authentic Mexican Shrimp Ceviche Recipe

Welcome to the vibrant world of Mexican cuisine, where fresh ingredients and bold flavors reign supreme! Today, we're diving into a beloved classic: **Authentic Mexican Shrimp Ceviche**. This dish, known as Ceviche de Camaron, is a staple along the coasts of Mexico, celebrated for its refreshing taste and simple preparation. It's the perfect appetizer or light meal, especially on a warm day, showcasing the incredible versatility of seafood when paired with bright citrus.

Our recipe guides you through making the best shrimp ceviche at home, ensuring an authentic experience bursting with tang, freshness, and a gentle kick of spice. Forget complicated cooking methods; here, the magic happens through the natural acidity of fresh lime juice, which 'cooks' the shrimp to tender perfection. Get ready to impress your friends and family with this easy shrimp ceviche recipe that truly captures the spirit of Mexico!

## What is Shrimp Ceviche?

Shrimp Ceviche, or Ceviche de Camaron, is a traditional Latin American seafood dish where fresh, raw fish or shellfish is cured in citrus juices, most commonly lime. The acid in the citrus denatures the proteins in the seafood, effectively 'cooking' it without heat. In Mexico, this dish is a culinary cornerstone, varying slightly from region to region but always emphasizing freshness and vibrant flavors. A classic Mexican **Shrimp Ceviche** typically features shrimp marinated in lime juice, then mixed with diced vegetables like tomato, onion, cilantro, and often a touch of spicy serrano or jalapeño pepper.

It's a testament to simple, clean eating, providing a delightful balance of sour, savory, and spicy notes. This preparation method not only preserves the delicate texture of the shrimp but also infuses it with an incredible zest that is undeniably Mexican. Understanding what is shrimp ceviche is the first step to appreciating its unique place in culinary traditions.

## Ingredients for Authentic Ceviche de Camaron

Crafting an authentic Ceviche de Camaron hinges on the quality and freshness of your ingredients. For the star of the show, you’ll need one pound of fresh, raw shrimp, peeled and deveined. Medium to large shrimp work best, cut into bite-sized pieces for optimal marination. The most crucial ingredient is fresh lime juice – about a cup, squeezed from 8-10 ripe limes. Never use bottled lime juice; the flavor simply won't compare for the best shrimp ceviche.

Complementing the shrimp are finely diced red onion, ripe Roma tomatoes (seeded to avoid excess moisture), and crisp cucumber. A generous half-cup of fresh cilantro, finely chopped, adds that quintessential herbaceous note. For a touch of heat, one or two finely minced serrano peppers are essential, adjusted to your spice preference. Salt and pepper will round out the seasoning, and a ripe avocado, diced, is a must for serving, adding a creamy contrast. These components come together to form a truly authentic Mexican shrimp ceviche.

## How to Make Mexican Shrimp Ceviche

Making Mexican **Shrimp Ceviche** is surprisingly straightforward, a testament to its fresh ingredients. Begin by preparing your shrimp: peel, devein, rinse, pat dry, and cut into uniform 1/2-inch pieces. Place the shrimp in a non-reactive bowl and cover completely with freshly squeezed lime juice. This is where the magic happens; the lime 'cooks' the shrimp. Refrigerate for 30-45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the shrimp turn pink and opaque. While the shrimp marinates, finely dice your red onion, tomatoes, cucumber, cilantro, and serrano pepper.

Once the shrimp is ready, drain about half of the excess lime juice. Add all the prepared vegetables to the shrimp mixture. Season generously with salt and pepper, then stir everything together gently. For the full flavor experience, refrigerate for another 15-20 minutes to allow the flavors to meld. This step-by-step guide ensures you get an authentic Mexican shrimp ceviche every time.

## Tips for the Best Shrimp Ceviche

To ensure your **Shrimp Ceviche** is truly exceptional, heed these expert tips. Firstly, always prioritize the freshest possible shrimp. Look for shrimp that are firm, translucent, and have a mild, oceanic smell. Freshly squeezed lime juice is non-negotiable; bottled juice lacks the vibrant acidity and complex flavor needed for authentic ceviche. When dicing your vegetables, strive for uniform, small pieces. This ensures every bite has a balanced medley of flavors and textures, making it an easy shrimp ceviche recipe to perfect.

Don't over-marinate the shrimp; 30-45 minutes is usually sufficient. Over-marinating can result in rubbery shrimp. Taste and adjust your seasoning frequently. The balance of salt, lime, and spice is key. Finally, add the avocado just before serving to prevent it from browning and to maintain its creamy texture. Following these tips will guarantee you the best shrimp ceviche at home.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Authentic Mexican **Shrimp Ceviche** is a dish meant for sharing and enjoying with complementary accompaniments. The most traditional way to serve Ceviche de Camaron is alongside crispy tostadas or tortilla chips. These provide the perfect crunchy vehicle for scooping up the zesty seafood mixture. For an added layer of flavor and texture, a dollop of creamy avocado or a few slices of ripe avocado on top of each tostada is highly recommended.

Pair this refreshing dish with a cold Mexican cerveza, a crisp white wine like Sauvignon Blanc, or a non-alcoholic agua fresca (such as jamaica or horchata). A side of Mexican rice or a simple green salad can also round out the meal beautifully. Whether served as an appetizer or a light main course, this authentic Mexican shrimp ceviche is always a crowd-pleaser, perfect for gatherings or a simple family meal.

## Variations & Substitutions

While this recipe provides a blueprint for authentic Mexican **Shrimp Ceviche**, there are several delightful variations and substitutions you can explore. For a different texture, you can briefly blanch the shrimp in boiling water for 1-2 minutes before marinating in lime juice; this is common in some coastal regions. If serrano peppers are too spicy, a milder jalapeño can be used, or omit it entirely for a non-spicy version. For an extra layer of flavor, a dash of clamato juice (spicy tomato and clam juice cocktail) is sometimes added, particularly in Baja California-style ceviche.

Feel free to experiment with other crisp vegetables like jicama or bell peppers for added crunch and color. While shrimp is classic, you can also make ceviche with firm white fish like snapper or mahi-mahi, or even mixed seafood. Remember, the key to any great ceviche, including this ceviche de camaron recipe, is using the freshest ingredients and balancing the vibrant citrus with your chosen additions.
